title: A protocol for FlyFos clone amplification
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"hwhes","text":"This is a protocol for amplification of pFlyFos clones.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Inoculate 50 ml of LB+Cm25 with a single colony of FlyFos strain. Culture overnight at 370;C with vigorous shaking. NOTE: Please follow 5B2; or 5CF; symbols throughout the protocol for options tailored to different volumes.

Use 5B2; 2 x 1 ml or 5CF; 2 x 5 ml to inoculate 5B2; 2 x 100 ml or 5CF; 2 x 500 ml LB+Cm25+Ara0.1% in 5B2; 500 ml or 5CF; 2500 ml flasks. Culture overnight at 370;C. Shake cultures vigorously 013; minimum 250 rpm.
Harvest the bacterial cells by centrifugation at 6000 x g for 15 min at 40;C.
Resuspend the bacterial pellet from both flasks combined in 5B2; 8 ml or 5CF; 50 ml of Buffer P1.
Add 5B2; 8 ml or 5CF; 50 ml of Buffer P2, mix thoroughly by vigorously inverting 4013;6 times, and incubate at room temperature for 5 min.
Add 5B2; 8 ml or 5CF; 50 ml of chilled Buffer P3, mix immediately and thoroughly by vigorously inverting 4013;6 times, and incubate on ice for 30 min.
Centrifuge at 265;20,000 x g for 30 min at 40;C. Remove supernatant containing plasmid DNA promptly.
Place folded Whatmann filter in a 50 ml syringe. Prewet and compress filter by passing water through the syringe. Use such prepared syringe for filtering supernatant.

Precipitate the DNA by adding 5B2; 17 ml or 5CF; 105 ml (0.7 volumes) of room temperature isopropanol to the lysate. Centrifuge at 265;15,000 x g for 30 min at 40;C, and carefully decant the supernatant.

Redissolve the DNA pellet in 500 BC;l warm (600;C) TE buffer, pH 8.0, and add Buffer QBT to obtain a final volume of 5B2; 5 ml or 5CF; 12 ml for selected 5B2; QIAGEN-tip 100 or 5CF; QIAGEN-tip 500, respectively.
Equilibrate a 5B2; QIAGEN-tip 100 or 5CF; QIAGEN-tip 500 by applying 5B2; 4 ml or 5CF; 10 ml Buffer QBT, and allow the column to empty by gravity flow.
Apply the DNA solution from step 10 to the QIAGEN-tip and allow it to enter the resin by gravity flow.
Wash the QIAGEN-tip with 5B2; 2 x 10 ml or 5CF; 2 x 30 ml Buffer QC.
Elute DNA with 5B2; 5 ml or 5CF; 15 ml Buffer QF.

Precipitate DNA by adding 5B2; 3.5 ml or 5CF; 10.5 ml (0.7 volumes) of room temperature isopropanol to the eluted DNA. Mix and centrifuge immediately at 265;15,000 x g for 30 min at 40;C. Carefully decant the supernatant.
Wash DNA pellet with 5B2; 2 ml or 5CF; 5 ml room-temperature 70% ethanol, and centrifuge at 265;15,000 x g for 10 min. Carefully decant the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.
Wash DNA pellet again with 5B2; 2 ml or 5CF; 5 ml room-temperature 70% ethanol, and centrifuge at 265;15,000 x g for 10 min. Carefully decant the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.
Air-dry the pellet for 5013;10 min, and redissolve the DNA in a suitable volume (5B2; 50 BC;l or 5CF; 250 BC;l) of warm (600;C) nucleases-free water.
You should obtain in total 5B2; 100 BC;g or 5CF; 500 BC;g of pure injection-quality fosmid DNA.

A toolkit for high-throughput, cross-species gene engineering in Drosophila by Radoslaw K Ejsmont, Mihail Sarov, Sylke Winkler, Kamil A Lipinski & Pavel Tomancak published in Nature Methods on 06 May, 2009 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.1334
